name: Sun Bin
description: Sun Bin, a brilliant military strategist of the Warring States period in Chinese history (c. 4th century BCE), is renowned for his strategic genius and tactical innovations despite facing immense personal tragedy. Originally a student alongside Pang Juan under the tutelage of the recluse Guiguzi, Sun Bin surpassed Pang Juan in skill, igniting Pang Juan's jealousy. Pang Juan, serving in the state of Wei, tricked Sun Bin into joining him, then falsely accused him of treason and had him brutally crippled, his kneecaps removed, leaving him permanently disabled. Feigning madness to survive, Sun Bin eventually escaped to the state of Qi. There, he was discovered by the Qi general Tian Ji, who recognized his extraordinary intellect. Sun Bin became Tian Ji's strategist, devising plans that secured victories against Wei, including the famous Battle of Guiling, where he exploited Wei's overconfidence and used a 'besiege Wei to rescue Zhao' tactic. His most celebrated triumph was at the Battle of Maling, where he lured Pang Juan and the Wei army into an ambush, resulting in Pang Juan's death and a decisive victory for Qi. Sun Bin's military theories and strategies are preserved in the 'Sun Bin's Art of War,' a foundational text of Chinese military thought, emphasizing deception, exploiting enemy weaknesses, and adapting to changing circumstances. His life exemplifies resilience, strategic brilliance, and the triumph of intellect over adversity. He is considered a pivotal figure in Chinese military history, whose teachings continue to influence modern strategic thinking.
subtitle: Crippled strategist, unmatched intellect.
achievements: Victory at the Battle of Guiling
Victory at the Battle of Maling
Developed the 'besiege Wei to rescue Zhao' tactic

keyMyths.title: The Crippling of Sun Bin
keyMyths.description: Pang Juan, jealous of Sun Bin's superior intellect, falsely accused him of treason and had his kneecaps removed, rendering him crippled. This act of betrayal fueled Sun Bin's desire for revenge and shaped his strategic thinking.

keyMyths.title: Besiege Wei to Rescue Zhao
keyMyths.description: During the Battle of Guiling, Sun Bin advised Tian Ji to attack the Wei capital instead of directly engaging the Wei army besieging Zhao. This forced Wei to withdraw and saved Zhao, showcasing Sun Bin's strategic brilliance.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Maling Ambush
keyMyths.description: Sun Bin lured Pang Juan's army into a narrow gorge at Maling, setting up a devastating ambush. He marked a tree with the words 'Pang Juan dies here' and waited for Pang Juan to arrive, resulting in Pang Juan's death and a major victory for Qi.

worshipPractices.name: Ancestral Veneration
worshipPractices.description: Sun Bin is honored as a significant ancestor and military strategist. Families and military personnel may offer prayers and tributes at ancestral shrines or temples, seeking his blessings for wisdom, strategic thinking, and victory in challenging situations.

cultural.modernLegacy: Sun Bin's strategies continue to be studied in military academies and business schools. He remains a symbol of strategic thinking, resilience, and overcoming adversity. His tactics are referenced in modern literature, film, and strategic planning.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Betrayal
extendedContent.content: Sun Bin's early life is shrouded in some mystery, but it is generally accepted that he was a descendant of Sun Tzu, the author of 'The Art of War'. He and Pang Juan studied military strategy together under the tutelage of the recluse Guiguzi. Sun Bin quickly proved to be the more talented of the two, a fact that ignited Pang Juan's jealousy. When Pang Juan gained a prominent position in the state of Wei, he invited Sun Bin to join him, ostensibly to share his success. However, upon Sun Bin's arrival, Pang Juan falsely accused him of treason. As punishment, Sun Bin was subjected to the brutal act of having his kneecaps removed, rendering him crippled for life. He was then imprisoned, and branded as a criminal, all in an attempt by Pang Juan to ensure that Sun Bin could never become a rival. This act of treachery set the stage for Sun Bin's eventual quest for revenge and his rise to legendary status.

extendedContent.title: Strategic Brilliance and the Battles of Guiling and Maling
extendedContent.content: Feigning madness to escape Pang Juan's clutches, Sun Bin eventually found refuge in the state of Qi. There, he was recognized for his strategic genius and appointed as a military advisor. His brilliance was first demonstrated at the Battle of Guiling, where he employed a strategy of 'besieging Wei to rescue Zhao'. By attacking the Wei capital, he forced Pang Juan to divert his forces, relieving the pressure on Zhao and inflicting a significant defeat on Wei. Sun Bin's most famous victory came at the Battle of Maling. Knowing Pang Juan's arrogance and predictable nature, Sun Bin laid a trap. He ordered his troops to decrease the number of cooking fires each day, leading Pang Juan to believe that the Qi army was deserting. Eager for a decisive victory, Pang Juan recklessly pursued them, only to be ambushed in a narrow pass. Pang Juan, realizing he was defeated, committed suicide. This victory not only avenged Sun Bin's personal suffering but also established Qi as a dominant power.

extendedContent.title: Legacy of Sun Bin
extendedContent.content: Sun Bin's legacy extends far beyond his military victories. His strategic principles, emphasizing deception, adaptability, and understanding the enemy, continue to be studied by military strategists and business leaders alike. His tactical innovations, such as the use of psychological warfare and the exploitation of enemy weaknesses, have had a lasting impact on military doctrine. Sun Bin's story is a testament to the power of intellect and resilience in the face of adversity. He transformed personal tragedy into strategic advantage, demonstrating that even the most crippling setbacks can be overcome with cunning and determination. His work, 'Sun Bin's Art of War', rediscovered in the 20th century, provides valuable insights into his strategic thinking and solidifies his place as one of history's greatest military minds. He remains a symbol of strategic brilliance, and a reminder that true strength lies not only in physical prowess but also in the ability to think strategically and adapt to changing circumstances.

shortDescription: Sun Bin was a brilliant, though physically disabled, Chinese military strategist whose cunning led to key victories and revenge on his betrayer, Pang Juan.
